item.title: Trumps says agreed to more Iran talks but insists truce over
item.description: US President Donald Trump said on Friday he had agreed to further negotiations with Iran, even as he repeated his assertion that the ceasefire between the decades-old foes was over. While there was no repeat of the previous day's strikes by early Friday evening in the Gulf, Iran reiterated its determination to hit back if attacked.

item.title: Mojtaba Khamenei absence signals shifting role for Iran leader
item.description: The absence of Iran's supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei from the funeral ceremonies of Ali Khamenei raised questions about his health and fear of assassination, as well as indicating he may perform a quite different function as number one compared with his all-powerful father. "The balance of power between the office of the supreme leader and the IRGC has shifted," he told AFP. Brodsky described Mojtaba Khamenei as a "weaker leader" than his father but noted it took Ali Khamenei "years to consolidate his authority" after being named in 1989.

item.title: Turkey says it hopes to achieve result on lifting U.S. sanctions soon
item.description:
ANKARA, July 10 (Reuters) - Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan said on Friday that Turkey hopes to achieve a result on lifting U.S. sanctions and its desire to purchase F-35 fighter jets soon, adding there should be no defence industry restrictions between allies.

U.S. President Donald Trump, who visited Ankara this week for a NATO Summit, announced on Tuesday that he would lift U.S. sanctions imposed on Turkey over its purchase of Russian S-400 defence missile systems.

item.title: Polish nationalists protest Jewish pogrom commemoration
item.description: Poland's Jewish community, political leaders and ordinary citizens on Friday commemorated a massacre of hundreds of Jews killed by their Polish neighbours 85 years ago, as far-right activists protested nearby. Among the organisers of the protest was the far-right Confederation of the Polish Crown party whose leader is Grzegorz Braun.

item.title: Qatari delegation is in Iran, aims to boost mediation role, Tasnim news agency says
item.description:
July 10 (Reuters) - A Qatari delegation visited Iran on Friday in what is believed to be an effort by Doha to consolidate its role as a mediator after a recent escalation in hostilities in the Gulf, Iran's semi-official Tasnim news agency reported.

Tasnim said the visit followed what it described as Qatari accusations against Iran over an alleged incident in the Strait of Hormuz and subsequent U.S. attacks on Iranian military and civilian targets.

item.title: Analysis-How the absence of Iran's new supreme leader is becoming a liability for the Islamic Republic
item.description:
By Parisa Hafezi and Angus McDowall

DUBAI, July 10 - The whereabouts of Mojtaba Khamenei have been a mystery to Iranians and the rest of the world alike since his appointment as supreme leader a week after the strike that killed his father at the end of February.

His absence from the main funeral ceremonies for former leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was so complete there was not even a written message, leaving people to guess at his plans for Iran at a turbulent time in the Islamic Republic's 47-year history.

item.title: Iran has asked to continue talks and the US agreed, Trump says
item.description:
July 10 (Reuters) - President Donald Trump on Friday said the United States had agreed to talks with Iran after Tehran asked to continue negotiations, while stressing that the June ceasefire between the two nations was over.

"The Islamic Republic of Iran has asked us to continue 'talks.' We have agreed to do so, but the United States has stated to them, in no uncertain terms, that the Cease Fire is OVER!" Trump wrote in a post on Truth Social.

item.title: Israel warns US of new Iran plot to assassinate Trump: What to know
item.description: US officials are reportedly still assessing the Israeli intelligence.

item.title: Lebanese PM Salam in Turkey as Erdogan seeks wider regional role
item.description: Lebanese Prime Minister Nawaf Salam’s visit to Turkey comes after Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan recently warned that Israeli attacks against Lebanon and Syria threaten Turkey’s security.

item.title: Lebanese PM Salam in Turkey as Erdogan seeks wider regional role
item.description: Lebanese Prime Minister Nawaf Salam’s visit to Turkey comes after Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan recently warned that Israeli attacks against Lebanon and Syria threaten Turkey’s security.

item.description: Lebanese Prime Minister Nawaf Salam’s visit to Turkey comes after Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan recently warned that Israeli attacks against Lebanon and Syria threaten Turkey’s security.

item.title: Countries must reject Iran efforts to control Hormuz, UN agency document says
item.description:
By Jonathan Saul

LONDON, July 10 (Reuters) - Countries should reject efforts by Iran to impose sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz and Tehran's "unilateral decision" to create a body to control traffic through the waterway, the U.N. shipping agency's governing council agreed on Friday.

The U.S. and Iran exchanged hostilities this week, including U.S. military airstrikes, prompted by attacks on ships that Washington said Tehran carried out.

item.title: In first, Russia confirms S-400 talks with Turkey amid transfer reports to acquire F-35
item.description: On Friday, the Kremlin spokesperson provided the first confirmation that Turkey and Russia have held discussions about Ankara's efforts to resolve the S-400 issue with Washington.

item.title: In first, Russia confirms S-400 talks with Turkey amid transfer reports to acquire F-35
item.description: On Friday, the Kremlin spokesperson provided the first confirmation that Turkey and Russia have held discussions about Ankara's efforts to resolve the S-400 issue with Washington.
